Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to compel compliance and production of documents is granted.

Plaintiff's mental health records are discoverable since her alleged emotional distress is an issue in this case. "[P]laintiffs . . . may not withhold information which relates to any physical or mental condition which they have put in issue by bringing th[e] lawsuit." (Britt v. Superior Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.) To the extent it has not already occurred, the court orders plaintiff to execute all required authorizations that are necessary for production of the documents identified in requests for production 1 - 7 within five days of notice of entry of this order. Alternatively, plaintiff may serve written confirmation that she has requested complete copies of records from each identified provider for the relevant period and executed any authorizations necessary for release, and (i) produce all responsive documents presently in her possession, custody, or control, and (ii) produce any additional responsive documents received from any provider within three business days of receipt, and in no event later than twenty-one calendar days from entry of the order, absent a declaration demonstrating good-faith efforts and provider-specific delay beyond Plaintiff's control.

Defendant contends that plaintiff has stonewalled the discovery process and acted without substantial justification. Defendant's notice of motion and the motion itself, however, do not seek monetary sanctions so the court awards none.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)

Motion For Entry Of Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Entry Of Protective Order.

Defendant's motion to enter protective order is granted. There is good cause for the order given the issues in this case (e.g. plaintiff's medical/mental health records defendant's investigations; personnel files). (Stadish v. Superior Court (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1130, 1145-1146; CCP 2017.020(a).)

The court determines that defendant's proposed order properly balances the interest of the parties and the public and adopts that form of order. (de Monet Decl., Ex. E.) Defendant shall e-mail a copy of that order to contestdept301tr@sftc.org for the court's signature. Sanctions denied.

Proposed order and proposed protective order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/BZ)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Na'il Benjamin, 510-897-9966 Benjamin Law Group for Plaintiff.
Adrienne De Monet, Esq. 424-302-6992 Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED. Parties state appearance. The Court announces the TR and states it received a timely notice to contest from Plaintiff. Ms. DeMonet aligns with the court's TR; Plaintiff's responses are deficient. Mr. Benjamin raises issues on interrogatory 207.2 and participation in the meet and confer. The Court denies Plaintiff' motion for leave to file a supplemental opposition. Ms. DeMonet responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories.

City and County of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories No 201.3, 203.1, 207.2, 213.2
